Subscribe [...] Gal Dayan

the whole reason people trust Light Phone is that it's deliberately incapable of most things, not just shaped like an old phone. curious where Flip lands on that spectrum - is this the same "as little as possible" philosophy in a flip shell, or does the nostalgia angle mean you've quietly added back more apps/features than the original Light Phone had, since that's usually how "fun retro form factor" products drift over a few versions.

Report [...] Promoted

creighton Nelson Vance

📌

Time and time again, we‘ve heard from people – especially the younger generations - who love flip phones, but the current options are poorly made, slow, unsupported, and often pre-installed with the same addictive apps built in. Light is taking everything that already works for tens of thousands of Light Phone users and is bringing that into a heavily desired yet timeless tactile form factor of a flip phone.

Gal Dayan

results.title: Instagram
results.content: There’s a nostalgia for a time before smartphones, when phones were just that. [...] Time and time again, we‘ve heard from people – especially the younger generations - who love flip phones, but the current options are poorly made, slow, unsupported, and often pre-installed with the same addictive apps built in. Light is taking everything that already works for tens of thousands of Light Phone users and is bringing that into a heavily-desired yet timeless tactile form factor of a flip phone.   Light Flip combines a modern, custom-built operating system (LightOS), durable hardware, and the same ethos that has defined our company: humanely designed phones that help people stay connected without constantly competing for their attention. All while still bringing the best aspects of technology; a headphone Jack, USB-C, bluetooth, wifi and 5G connectivity.

results.title: Why I switched back to a flip phone (as a gen z) 🔅
results.content: spend that much time on this phone compared to a smartphone just because it's less convenient right when you once had the convenience it's really hard to go back for example just like the T9 function on this keyboard like how to type right if you're not familiar with T9 there are two ways to type on it basically number one is just typing each individual letter so if you want to type a you would press two once if you want to type B you would press two twice and if you you want to type c you would press two three times and there's also the other way which is typing the corresponding number to each letter of the word for example if you want to type hello you would look at the four letters right so H would be on number pad four and E would be on three and then L would be on five and then o [...] keep up with it and sometimes even if you want to go back it's hard to I didn't know how much technology had control over our lives I always thought it was the other way around so just having this whole flipped phone has opened a new IE for me and yeah that's basically all I wanted to say in this video like yeah I know it's a pretty random video for me to make cuz most of my videos are more related to art and drawing I know a lot of you guys probably kicked on it just like my initial reason just because of the Aesthetics but yes it looks really cute and I think a lot of us right now are all about that Nostalgia YK Factor right now which I totally relate and understand to but I do think it's a very interesting topic not just cuz my flip phone is so cute right but it's also just like our [...] survive in this day and age without my smartphone like a lot of the banking apps I use are on my smartphone and they are not compatible with my flip phone another thing is for example especially in Asia when you transport a lot of the transport cards are on the phone not just that even going to restaurant sometimes you use your QR code to order and they don't even provide the physical menu sometimes but it's especially in Asia I feel like sometimes like you need your smartphone to go out for like the simplest things like everything is on your smartphone now which I feel like it is a busting and a curse but that's also why as much as I want to I cannot get rid of my smartphone completely and it is still there another thing I found interesting okay like lifestyle changes of course

results.title: Instagram
results.content:
Not everyone is convinced, though. Critics argue that the movement could simply be another short-lived aesthetic trend rather than a lasting lifestyle shift.  
  
Whether it's about mental health, nostalgia, or escaping endless notifications, one thing is clear—the humble flip phone is making an unexpected comeback.  
  
What do you think—could you switch back to a dumb phone?  
  
Video courtesy - @sahaschopra   
.  
.  
.  
[Gen Z, flip phone, dumb phone, digital detox, smartphones, vintage tech, early 2000s, screen time, mental health, nostalgia, technology, social media, minimalism, trend, lifestyle]  
  
#GenZ #DigitalDetox #FlipPhone #Technology #Lifestyle [...] Is the smartphone era losing its grip on Gen Z?  
  
A growing number of Gen Z users are turning away from smartphones and embracing early 2000s flip phones, dumb phones, and vintage cameras.  
  
While these devices lack modern apps and social media features, many young people say they offer something more valuable—less screen time, fewer notifications, and better focus.  
  
The trend has gained momentum across social media, with many describing it as a form of digital detox and a way to improve mental well-being.  
  
 Others see it as a mix of nostalgia, minimalism, and a desire to disconnect from the constant pressure of staying online. [...] Log In
